sourcingNotes.poolAssessment: Wikimedia Commons has 4-5 Bushrangers Bay images in the Mornington Peninsula National Park category. Key constraint: must not twin with home-cover-01 (which uses the Jerowa Two Bays Walking Track view, 4032x3024). Two distinct candidates survive twinning filter and editorial bar.
sourcingNotes.searchApproach: Commons API — Category:Mornington_Peninsula_National_Park, filtered for Bushrangers Bay subject, verified licence per-image, cross-checked twinning register against home-cover-01 candidate C.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY-SA 4.0. Own work. SA override required.
candidates.place: Bushrangers Bay — view from Cape Schanck looking back to the bay
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: RECOMMENDED. 5184x3456 — excellent resolution with full crop headroom. Shot from Cape Schanck looking BACK to Bushrangers Bay, showing the distinctive rock stacks and bay geometry. Completely distinct composition from home-cover-01 (which looks DOWN into the bay from the walking track). Summer morning light (January). SA licence flag. Strong editorial weight — the elevated angle gives the bay its recognisable shape.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY-SA 4.0. Own work. SA override required.
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Alternative. 4160x2336 — good resolution, naturally wider aspect (close to 16:9). Beach-level perspective showing the bay and basalt cliffs. Different angle from candidate A and from home-cover-01. January light. SA licence. Less editorial impact than candidate A because the beach-level angle is more 'postcard' — but still genuinely Bushrangers Bay.

twinningRegister.home-cover-01: Uses Jerowa Two Bays Walking Track view (File:Bushrangers_Bay,_Mornington_Peninsula_National_Park_Victoria_Australia.jpeg). Neither candidate A nor B here twins with that image.
twinningRegister.explore-hub-hero-01: Uses Cape Schanck walking trail as fallback per dry-run shortlist. Candidate A here is from Cape Schanck area but at a different angle and subject (the bay, not the trail).

editorActionsRequested.question: Both candidates are CC-BY-SA 4.0. Contingent on blanket SA policy acceptance.
